mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals expanding supply capacity that translates into positive earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, restrictive monetary policy increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and a fall in retail sales signal joint labor market deterioration and demand contraction, forcing downward earnings revisions and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods where UNRATE rises month-over-month and RSAFS falls month-over-month jointly do not produce negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression has a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in housing starts during a monetary tightening cycle signal rapid transmission of higher mortgage rates into residential investment, foreshadowing broader demand weakness and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1382.0 and FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of early 2023
failure_condition: In months when FEDFUNDS is above 3%, declines in HOUST are followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate signal an aggressive inflation-fighting stance that raises real rate uncertainty, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS do not produce negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of joint-increase months.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points over a rolling three-month window signals weakening labor income and consumption expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp in 3 months, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail sales reflect resilient consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in RSAFS coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines month-over-month, it signals contracting manufacturing activity that drags on earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances.
